questions.question: Describe the difference between a waterfall model and an iterative model in software development. Provide an example to illustrate each approach.
questions.hint: Focus on the key characteristics of each model

questions.question: A software development project has 15 team members. The team is using an iterative model with 3 iterations. Calculate the total number of iterations required to complete the project.
questions.hint: Use the formula: total iterations = (number of team members * number of iterations) / 2

questions.question: Explain the concept of risk management in software engineering. Provide an example of a risk management strategy that can be applied in a software development project.
questions.hint: Focus on the importance of risk management in software development
